task_completion.description: The Segment Editor module is open and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module panel title reads 'Segment Editor' or the Segment Editor icon is highlighted in the toolbar.

task_completion.description: A new segment (e.g., 'NonBrain', 'Inverse', 'Background') has been added to the segment list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Segments' table, there are at least two rows: the original 'Brain' segment and a new second segment.

task_completion.description: The 'Logical operators' effect is selected in the Effects panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Logical operators' button (icon usually looks like set theory circles) is highlighted or its options (Operation: Invert, Subtract, etc.) are displayed below the effects grid.

task_completion.description: The new segment is visible in the slice views, covering the background and skull.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D slice views (Axial, Sagittal, or Coronal) show a colored overlay corresponding to the new segment that covers the dark background air and the skull area surrounding the brain.

task_completion.description: The combined segments cover the entire volume with the new segment strictly complementing the brain segment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Brain' segment (usually red/pink) and the new 'Inverse' segment (another color) together fill the entire rectangular slice view like a puzzle, with the new segment filling everything that is NOT the brain.

integrity.description: The screenshots show the actual 3D Slicer GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard 3D Slicer layout with the module panel on the left, slice views on the right, and standard toolbar icons.

integrity.description: The result is generated within Slicer, not by opening a pre-edited image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The window title or data module shows 'MRHead' as the active volume, and the segmentation is being edited live (e.g., Segment Editor controls are visible).

integrity.description: No blocking error messages or python exceptions are visible.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen is free of popup windows containing 'Error', 'Exception', or red warning icons.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the 'MRHead.nrrd' dataset. The original segment is named 'Brain'. The correct result requires a second segment (e.g., 'NonBrain') that acts as a boolean inverse: it must cover the skull and background air, leaving the brain region empty (or covered by the original segment). The two segments should have minimal overlap (<1%).
